September update, progress, forward...

Posted on September 13, 2020

This week I’ve almost completed filling the portfolio page with various projects that I’ve worked on. I must admit, this this process of structuring and organizing was quite enjoyable in the beginning, but as time passed, I started to mistakes. This became quite exhausting, that after filling out the final pieces I needed to take a break on work on something else.

There are still 2 projects, which I want to document one being my master thesis as this is where I also used different machine learning methods such as decision trees, random forests and soft decision trees. I found even nice code which I ported from the C++ implementation for easier readability. And the other one, is an implementation of stacked auto-encoders. This code was really early days for me as it is more or less 5 years ago. I will try to clean up the code, and make a tiny post about it the goals, motivations and how it could be used for something else.

Finishing the portfolio page is important for me because, then I can move on and write about some of my more recent tiny projects, which I’ve done for some companies in terms of simple regression, classification and perhaps even using different deep learning frameworks.

Furthermore, I managed to learn a bit more about jekyll and how this whole setup works. It is extremly time consuming, some very simple things just eat up time and take hours to figure out. I updated the links page with some additional sources.

The next week, I hope to finish the mentioned 2 projects, such that I could move on to some of more interesting subjects! Such as writing what’s currently hot topics, learn more how to update and add more features to the jekyll and so on!
